preamble: The shift from the alveolar nasal [n] to the uvular nasal [ɴ] is a form of retraction, often occurring in specific phonetic environments like the end of an utterance or before uvular consonants.

examples.note: The moraic nasal /N/ is realized as [ɴ] utterance-finally.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
